heading: Enforcement provisions
label: 2805 Enforcement provisions
notes: References in TextThe Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, referred to in subsec. (d)(1), are set out in the Appendix to Title 28, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.

children.heading: Maintenance of civil action by franchisee against franchisor; jurisdiction and venue; time for commencement of action
children.label: a Maintenance of civil action by franchisee against franchisor; jurisdiction and venue; time for commencement of action

children.text: the date of termination of the franchise or nonrenewal of the franchise relationship; or
the date the franchisor fails to comply with the requirements of section 2802, 2803, or 2807 of this title.

children.heading: Equitable relief by court; bond requirements; grounds for nonexercise of court’s equitable powers
children.label: b Equitable relief by court; bond requirements; grounds for nonexercise of court’s equitable powers

children.text: In any action under subsection (a), the court shall grant such equitable relief as the court determines is necessary to remedy the effects of any failure to comply with the requirements of section 2802, 2803, or 2807 of this title, including declaratory judgment, mandatory or prohibitive injunctive relief, and interim equitable relief.

children.label: i
children.text: the franchise of which he is a party has been terminated or the franchise relationship of which he is a party has not been renewed, and

children.label: ii
children.text: there exist sufficiently serious questions going to the merits to make such questions a fair ground for litigation; and

children.label: B
children.text: the court determines that, on balance, the hardships imposed upon the franchisor by the issuance of such preliminary injunctive relief will be less than the hardship which would be imposed upon such franchisee if such preliminary injunctive relief were not granted.

Nothing in this subsection prevents any court from requiring the franchisee in any action under subsection (a) to post a bond, in an amount established by the court, prior to the issuance or continuation of any equitable relief.

children.label: A
children.text: more than 90 days after the date on which notification pursuant to was posted or personally delivered to the franchisee;section 2804(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: more than 180 days after the date on which notification pursuant to was posted or personally delivered to the franchisee; orsection 2804(b)(2) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: more than 30 days after the date on which the termination of such franchise or the nonrenewal of such franchise relationship takes effect if less than 90 days notification was provided pursuant to .section 2804(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Burden of proof; burden of going forward with evidence
children.label: c Burden of proof; burden of going forward with evidence
children.text: In any action under subsection (a), the franchisee shall have the burden of proving the termination of the franchise or the nonrenewal of the franchise relationship. The franchisor shall bear the burden of going forward with evidence to establish as an affirmative defense that such termination or nonrenewal was permitted under section 2802(b) or 2803 of this title, and, if applicable, that such franchisor complied with the requirements of .section 2802(d) of this title

children.heading: Actual and exemplary damages and attorney and expert witness fees to franchisee; determination by court of right to exemplary damages and amount; attorney and expert witness fees to franchisor for frivolous actions
children.label: d Actual and exemplary damages and attorney and expert witness fees to franchisee; determination by court of right to exemplary damages and amount; attorney and expert witness fees to franchisor for frivolous actions
A
children.text: consistent with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, to actual damages;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any such action which is based upon conduct of the franchisor which was in willful disregard of the requirements of section 2802, 2803, or 2807 of this title, or the rights of the franchisee thereunder, to exemplary damages, where appropriate; and

children.label: C
children.text: to reasonable attorney and expert witness fees to be paid by the franchisor, unless the court determines that only nominal damages are to be awarded to such franchisee, in which case the court, in its discretion, need not direct that such fees be paid by the franchisor.
The question of whether to award exemplary damages and the amount of any such award shall be determined by the court and not by a jury.

In any action under subsection (a), the court may, in its discretion, direct that reasonable attorney and expert witness fees be paid by the franchisee if the court finds that such action is frivolous.

children.heading: Discretionary power of court to compel continuation or renewal of franchise relationship; grounds for noncompulsion; right of franchisee to actual damages and attorney and expert witness fees unaffected
children.label: e Discretionary power of court to compel continuation or renewal of franchise relationship; grounds for noncompulsion; right of franchisee to actual damages and attorney and expert witness fees unaffected
i
children.text: to convert the leased marketing premises to a use other than the sale or distribution of motor fuel,

children.label: ii
children.text: to materially alter, add to, or replace such premises,

children.label: iii
children.text: to sell such premises,

children.label: iv
children.text: to withdraw from the marketing of motor fuel through retail outlets in the relevant geographic market area in which the marketing premises are located, or

children.label: v
children.text: that renewal of the franchise relationship is likely to be uneconomical to the franchisor despite any reasonable changes or reasonable additions to the provisions of the franchise which may be acceptable to the franchisee; and

children.label: B
children.text: the requirements of have been complied with.section 2804 of this title

The provisions of paragraph (1) shall not affect any right of any franchisee to recover actual damages and reasonable attorney and expert witness fees under subsection (d) if such nonrenewal is prohibited by .section 2802 of this title

children.heading: Release or waiver of rights
children.label: f Release or waiver of rights
A
children.text: any right that the franchisee has under this subchapter or other Federal law; or

children.label: B
children.text: any right that the franchisee may have under any valid and applicable State law.

No provision of any franchise shall be valid or enforceable if the provision specifies that the interpretation or enforcement of the franchise shall be governed by the law of any State other than the State in which the franchisee has the principal place of business of the franchisee.
